location.name: Holviken
location.weather: low chalk-white fog sitting on the water

crime.cause_of_death: a thin blade between the ribs
crime.time_of_death: between ten at night and midnight
crime.hidden_truth: The victim had discovered Sigrid and the lighthouse keeper meeting secretly and had written it down in a ledger before he was killed.

characters.name: Nils Vander
characters.description: Nils was the village's chalk merchant, a meticulous man in his fifties known for keeping careful records. His body lies face-down in the tidal silt, boots still on, coat undone at the collar, one arm stretched toward the sea.

characters.name: Sigrid Arnell
characters.description: Sigrid is Nils Vander's business partner and the widow of a sea captain; she manages the chalk-export accounts with a composed efficiency that unsettles people who expect grief. She meets the investigator at her door with dry hands and a steady gaze, a narrow gutting knife hanging from her belt in its sheath.

characters.name: Ebbe Strand
characters.description: Ebbe is the lighthouse keeper, a lean man in his late thirties with calloused palms and a habit of looking past whoever he speaks to. He is polite when the investigator climbs the lighthouse steps, though his eyes move to the window and the fog beyond.

characters.name: Marte Okkel
characters.description: Marte runs the harbourmaster's office and was the one who sent for the investigator after the body was found by a fisherman at dawn. She is a broad-shouldered woman in her forties with ink on her fingers, and she speaks quickly, as if she has already rehearsed what she will and will not say.

characters.name: Per Haas
characters.description: Per is the chalk barn's labourer, young, sun-browned, and visibly frightened; he was sleeping in the barn's loft the night of the murder and claims he heard nothing. His hands shake slightly when he gestures toward the stacked chalk blocks.

characters.name: Idunn Vander
characters.description: Idunn is Nils Vander's daughter, twenty years old, and has worked as a clerk in the chalk business since she left school. She is sitting at Sigrid's kitchen table when the investigator arrives, her eyes red, a cup of untouched tea before her.

items.name: Account Ledger
items.description: A thick cloth-bound ledger, pages water-swollen at the edges; the final two pages have been torn out, leaving ragged paper stubs at the spine.

items.name: Gutting Knife
items.description: A thin-bladed knife, roughly twenty centimetres long, with a bone handle worn smooth; a dark reddish-brown stain runs along the fuller near the hilt.

items.name: Chalk-Dusted Boot Print
items.description: A single dried boot print pressed into the mud at the barn threshold, caked with white chalk dust on its sole-edge.

items.name: Torn Ledger Pages
items.description: Two loose pages of dense handwritten columns, damp and partially smeared; they were folded and tucked beneath a loose floorboard.

items.name: Lantern Oil Stain
items.description: A palm-sized dark stain on the chalk barn's earthen floor near the west wall, with a small crescent of broken glass pressed into the soil beside it.

items.name: Damp Wool Coat
items.description: A heavy wool coat hanging on a peg inside the lighthouse door, its lower hem and sleeves still damp and smelling of tidal mud.

locations.name: Harbour Steps
locations.flavour: Broad stone steps descend from the village into the grey harbour water; lobster pots and coiled rope line the upper landing.

locations.name: Tidal Flats
locations.flavour: At low tide the sea retreats to reveal a wide shelf of grey-white silt criss-crossed with boot marks and the tracks of wading birds.

locations.name: Chalk Barn
locations.flavour: A long low building of tarred timber; the air inside is pale with chalk dust that coats every surface in a fine white powder.

locations.name: Arnell House
locations.flavour: A sea captain's house, solidly built and facing the water; the shutters are freshly painted but the garden has been left to go wild.

locations.name: Lighthouse
locations.flavour: The chalk-white tower stands on the northern headland, its beam cutting through the fog at irregular intervals the fishermen say they could set a clock by.

locations.name: Cliff Path
locations.flavour: A narrow track cut into the chalk headland connects the village to the lighthouse; the fog collects here and the path edge crumbles without warning.

locations.name: Harbourmaster's Office
locations.flavour: A single room above the harbour steps with salt-bleached charts pinned to every wall and a tide-table scratched directly into the windowsill.

checkpoints.label: Cause of Death
answer_options: a thin blade between the ribs

checkpoints.label: Time of Death
answer_options: between ten at night and midnight
between eight and ten at night
between midnight and two in the morning

checkpoints.label: Motive
answer_options: a dispute over the chalk business accounts
revenge for the death of a family member
a debt that could not be repaid

checkpoints.label: What Did the Victim Know?
answer_options: The victim had discovered Sigrid and the lighthouse keeper meeting secretly and had written it down in a ledger before he was killed.
The victim had uncovered evidence of fraud in the chalk export records.
The victim was planning to sell the chalk barn without Sigrid's consent.
The victim had witnessed a shipwreck being deliberately caused.
The victim was secretly the father of Idunn's child.

clues.answer: a thin blade between the ribs
clues.text: Examining the body where it lies in the silt, the investigator finds a single small entry wound on the left side of the torso, just below the fourth rib — narrow, clean-edged, no tearing of the surrounding cloth.

clues.answer: a thin blade between the ribs
clues.text: The blade is narrow — no wider than a finger — and tapers to a fine point; the stain near the hilt is dry and dark, and the edge shows no nicks or damage.
[char:Marte Okkel] tells the investigator: "The fisherman who found him said Nils was lying right at the water's edge, face down in the wet. The tide had been up past him not two hours before."
clues.red_herring_explanation: Marte is reporting the body's position truthfully, but drowning is not the cause of death; the location of the body reflects it having been moved, not how the victim died.

clues.text: [char:Per Haas] tells the investigator: "I heard a bang from below, sometime in the evening — like something heavy falling against the chalk blocks. I didn't go down."
clues.red_herring_explanation: Per heard the lantern being knocked over during the struggle in the barn; the sound was not a blow to the victim's head.

clues.text: [char:Idunn Vander] tells the investigator: "His collar was undone when they found him — Father never wore it that way. He was very particular about his appearance."
clues.red_herring_explanation: Idunn correctly observes the undone collar but draws no conclusion; the collar was loosened when the body was dragged, not as part of any struggle around the neck.

clues.text: The oil stain and broken glass sit on the earthen floor beside a smear of dark matter that extends several feet toward the barn's rear door; the chalk dust on the floor around it is disturbed in two broad arcs, as if something was dragged.

The boot print at the threshold faces outward — toward the tidal flats path — and the chalk dust on its sole-edge is heavier on the heel than the toe, as if the foot was moving under considerable weight.
[char:Marte Okkel] tells the investigator: "Nils walked the tidal flats most evenings — everyone knew it. If you wanted to find him alone after dark, that's where you'd go."
clues.red_herring_explanation: Marte is describing Nils's known habit accurately, but the murder did not occur on the tidal flats; the body was brought there afterward.

clues.text: [char:Idunn Vander] tells the investigator: "Father went to see Sigrid that evening — he told me before supper. He said he needed to go over the accounts with her and that he'd be back by nine."
clues.red_herring_explanation: Nils did visit Arnell House, but he and Sigrid moved to the chalk barn to retrieve the ledger; the murder took place in the barn, not the house.

clues.text: On the chalk path, the investigator finds a small piece of dark wool snagged on a gorse branch at chest height, and below it a partial boot print in the pale soil.
clues.red_herring_explanation: The wool and print belong to Ebbe Strand, who used the cliff path to visit Sigrid the previous night; they pre-date the murder and are not connected to the crime scene.

clues.answer: between ten at night and midnight
clues.text: [char:Per Haas] tells the investigator: "I heard the bang and then voices — short and quiet — sometime after the harbour bell struck ten. I fell back asleep before I heard the bell again."

clues.answer: between ten at night and midnight
clues.text: [char:Marte Okkel] tells the investigator: "The tide chart shows the flats were fully exposed by [time:half past eleven]. The body was left when the tide was already out — you can see from the silt marks where the water stopped, and it stopped well short of him."

clues.answer: between eight and ten at night
clues.text: [char:Idunn Vander] tells the investigator: "Father left the house just after supper — [time:around eight]. He didn't come home. I waited until ten and then I went to bed."
clues.red_herring_explanation: Idunn establishes when her father left, not when he died; the departure time is earlier than the murder window.

clues.text: [char:Ebbe Strand] tells the investigator: "I was watching the water from the lamp room [time:around four in the morning] and I saw nothing on the flats — no shape, no movement. Whatever was down there, I didn't see it until the fog thinned at first light."
clues.red_herring_explanation: Ebbe's testimony concerns visibility and the fog, not the time of death; the body was already there when he claims not to have seen it.

clues.answer: between midnight and two in the morning
clues.text: On the tide-table scratched into the windowsill, the investigator finds a pencil mark at [time:one in the morning] beside the notation "full ebb"; the scratch is recent, the pencil dust still loose.
clues.red_herring_explanation: The pencil mark was made by Marte working out when to send for the investigator; it refers to tide timing, not to the hour of death.

clues.text: The bone handle of the knife is worn on the right side in a pattern consistent with years of the same grip; a single dark hair is caught beneath the bolster, pressed flat as if trapped when the knife was last sheathed.

Asked directly, [char:Sigrid Arnell] admits she was at [loc:chalk_barn] that evening, saying she and Nils had gone to retrieve a ledger; her boots, still on her feet, carry a film of white chalk dust on the soles and lower heel.
[char:Per Haas] tells the investigator: "Nils was going to let me go — Sigrid told me last week. Said the business was shrinking and he'd be working the barn himself from winter."
clues.red_herring_explanation: Per has a reason to resent Nils and was present in the barn, but the murder weapon belongs to Sigrid and Per had no access to it.

clues.text: The coat is damp from hem to mid-sleeve with tidal mud; on the right cuff, there is a faint white powdery residue along the inside edge of the sleeve.
clues.red_herring_explanation: The coat is damp because Ebbe walked the tidal path the previous evening visiting Sigrid; the chalk residue is from the cliff path, not the chalk barn interior.

clues.text: On the desk, among the charts, there is a folded note in a hand the investigator does not recognise: "Tonight — after the bell — do not wait up." The note has no signature.
clues.red_herring_explanation: The note was sent to Marte by a fishing captain she was seeing; it has no connection to the murder.

clues.text: The torn pages contain rows of dates, initials, and times; two sets of initials — S.A. and E.S. — recur on the same dates across four months, always late at night, with a location notation that reads "L.H."

Shown the torn pages, [char:Ebbe Strand] goes very still; after a long pause he says: "Nils came to the lighthouse [time:three weeks ago]. He had the ledger with him. He said nothing — just looked at me and wrote something down."

clues.answer: a dispute over the chalk business accounts
clues.text: The intact pages of the ledger show a series of shipment entries where the weight recorded at dispatch is consistently higher than the weight recorded at receipt; the discrepancy runs across the last three months.
clues.red_herring_explanation: The weight discrepancies are a genuine bookkeeping irregularity Nils had noticed, but they are unrelated to why he was killed; the torn pages, not the account columns, were what Sigrid needed to destroy.

clues.text: [char:Marte Okkel] tells the investigator: "There have been unlicensed landings on the northern flats twice this autumn — no manifest, no duty paid. Nils logged them both in the harbourmaster's record. Someone knew."
clues.red_herring_explanation: Nils did log the unlicensed landings, but Sigrid killed him to protect the affair, not to stop him reporting smuggling; the landings are a separate matter Marte is handling.

clues.answer: a debt that could not be repaid
clues.text: [char:Idunn Vander] tells the investigator: "Father lent Sigrid money when Captain Arnell died — a considerable sum. She was supposed to repay it from the business profits, but she never did. Father mentioned it [time:last spring], and then he never mentioned it again."
clues.red_herring_explanation: The unpaid debt is a real grievance but it ran in Sigrid's favour, not against her; she had no financial motive to kill the man to whom she owed money.

clues.answer: The victim had discovered Sigrid and the lighthouse keeper meeting secretly and had written it down in a ledger before he was killed.
clues.text: Holding the torn pages inside the lighthouse, the investigator can see that the paper's watermark matches exactly the ledger found in the chalk barn; the pages slot back against the torn stubs with a precise fit.

clues.answer: The victim had discovered Sigrid and the lighthouse keeper meeting secretly and had written it down in a ledger before he was killed.
clues.text: Shown the torn pages, [char:Sigrid Arnell] says nothing for a long moment; then: "He kept records of everything. That was always his way."

clues.answer: The victim had uncovered evidence of fraud in the chalk export records.
clues.text: A pencilled annotation in the margin of the ledger reads: "Check Arnell receipts — weight variance consistent, not random," followed by a date [time:six weeks ago].
clues.red_herring_explanation: Nils did notice the weight variance and suspected something; but the torn pages show his primary concern was the affair, not fraud. The annotation reflects a parallel suspicion he never acted on.

clues.answer: The victim was planning to sell the chalk barn without Sigrid's consent.
clues.text: [char:Per Haas] tells the investigator: "I overheard Nils on the road [time:two weeks ago], speaking to a man from the town — something about a sale price and clearing the lease before winter."
clues.red_herring_explanation: Nils was discussing a separate property matter — a storage lot near the harbour — not the chalk barn itself. Per misunderstood what he overheard.

clues.answer: The victim had witnessed a shipwreck being deliberately caused.
clues.text: [char:Ebbe Strand] tells the investigator: "There was a vessel that went on the rocks [time:last spring] — the light was operating correctly. Some people in the village blamed me anyway. Nils was among them."
clues.red_herring_explanation: The shipwreck was an accident and Ebbe is sensitive about the accusation; but Nils's grievance never went beyond village gossip and is unrelated to the ledger entries.

leads.text: The chalk barn is the centre of Nils's working life — [char:Per Haas] sleeps there and [char:Sigrid Arnell] holds a key.
[char:Marte Okkel] was the first official to reach the body and has the tide charts for last night; she is at the [loc:harbour_steps].
The knife on [char:Sigrid Arnell]'s belt drew the fisherman's eye when she came to the harbour this morning — he noted it was usually kept in her kitchen.

opening_narrative:
The fog came in off the Kattemark shelf [time:two nights ago] and has not shifted since. In the village of Holviken, where the chalk cliffs run down to a tidal shelf and the fishermen read the lighthouse beam like a heartbeat, fog is ordinary. The body of [char:Nils Vander], chalk merchant, found face-down in the grey silt of the [loc:tidal_flats] at first light — that was not.

[char:Marte Okkel], harbourmaster, sent the message herself, bypassing the district constable entirely. Her note was brief: a man of standing found dead in circumstances she did not wish to characterise, a village that would not speak freely to its own, and a request for someone from outside. She included the tide table as an attachment, which suggested she was already thinking carefully.

The investigator arrives by the morning packet at the [loc:harbour_steps], the fog still sitting low on the water. [char:Marte Okkel] is waiting on the upper landing, her coat buttoned to the throat, a rolled chart under one arm. She says only: "He was well-liked here. That will make it harder." Then she turns and walks back toward the village, leaving the investigator to follow.
